ucs.title: [LLM] Whitefly Vcrodat-style DLL search-order hijack via signed security-vendor binaries
ucs.description: Hunts the signature Whitefly tradecraft: dropping a legitimate signed executable (frequently from security vendors such as F-Secure, Symantec, Sophos, Kaspersky) into a writable, non-standard directory alongside a malicious unsigned DLL that is side-loaded as 'Vcrodat'. Combination of legitimate-signed-EXE + unsigned-sibling-DLL + non-standard path is characteristic of the loader Symantec attributed to Whitefly in the SingHealth-linked intrusions.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process_path) as process_path values(Processes.process) as cmdline from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_signature_status="Signed" AND (Processes.process_publisher IN ("*F-Secure*","*Symantec*","*Sophos*","*Kaspersky*","*Trend Micro*","*McAfee*","*ESET*","*Bitdefender*")) AND NOT (Processes.process_path IN ("*\\Program Files\\*","*\\Program Files (x86)\\*","*\\Windows\\System32\\*")) by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process_name Processes.process_guid | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | join type=inner process_guid [| tstats summariesonly=true count from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where Filesystem.file_name="*.dll" AND Filesystem.file_path!="*\\Program Files*" AND Filesystem.file_path!="*\\Windows\\System32*" by Filesystem.process_guid Filesystem.file_path Filesystem.file_name | rename Filesystem.process_guid as process_guid Filesystem.file_path as dll_path Filesystem.file_name as dll_name] | where like(dll_path, substr(process_path,1,len(process_path)-len(process_name))) | eval risk_reason="Signed AV-vendor binary loaded from non-standard path with sibling unsigned DLL — Whitefly Vcrodat pattern" | table firstTime lastTime dest user process_name process_path dll_name dll_path risk_reason | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)`
ucs.kql:
let SuspectVendors = dynamic(["F-Secure","Symantec","Sophos","Kaspersky Lab","Trend Micro","McAfee","ESET","Bitdefender"]);
let SignedVendorBinariesOffPath = DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(30d)
| where InitiatingProcessSignatureStatus == "Valid" or ProcessVersionInfoCompanyName has_any (SuspectVendors)
| where ProcessVersionInfoCompanyName has_any (SuspectVendors)
| where FolderPath !startswith "C:\\Program Files" and FolderPath !startswith "C:\\Program Files (x86)" and FolderPath !startswith "C:\\Windows\\System32"
| project Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName, ProcessId, ProcessGuid=ProcessId, ProcessFolder=tostring(split(FolderPath,FileName)[0]), FileName, ProcessVersionInfoCompanyName;
let UnsignedSiblingDlls = DeviceImageLoadEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(30d)
| where FileName endswith ".dll"
| where SHA256 != "" 
| where InitiatingProcessFolderPath !startswith "C:\\Program Files" and InitiatingProcessFolderPath !startswith "C:\\Windows\\System32"
| project Timestamp, DeviceId, ImageFolder=tostring(split(FolderPath,FileName)[0]), DllName=FileName, DllSha256=SHA256, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessId;
SignedVendorBinariesOffPath
| join kind=inner UnsignedSiblingDlls on DeviceId
| where ProcessFolder =~ ImageFolder
| join kind=leftouter (DeviceFileCertificateInfo | project DllSha256=SHA256, IsTrusted, IsSigned) on DllSha256
| where IsSigned == false or IsTrusted == false or isempty(IsSigned)
| extend Detection = "Whitefly Vcrodat side-load: signed AV-vendor EXE in user-writable path with unsigned sibling DLL"
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, FileName, ProcessFolder, DllName, DllSha256, ProcessVersionInfoCompanyName, Detection
ucs.rationale: Symantec's reporting on Whitefly's SingHealth-linked operations specifically calls out the 'Vcrodat' loader, which is delivered by abusing legitimate, signed security-vendor executables to side-load a malicious DLL. The exact chain of (signed vendor EXE) + (off-path location) + (sibling unsigned DLL) is the actor's hallmark — generic DLL hijack rules miss the vendor-binary specificity, and generic

ucs.title: [LLM] Whitefly masqueraded Mimikatz LSASS access from non-System32 paths
ucs.description: Hunts Whitefly's documented use of Mimikatz with file-name masquerading (T1036.005) to dump LSASS (T1003.001). Whitefly is known to rename open-source credential-theft tooling to mimic Windows-native binaries (svchost.exe, lsass.exe, taskhost.exe, services.exe) and execute them from staging directories such as ProgramData, Public, and Temp. Detection ties the LSASS handle/dump to a process whose name impersonates a system binary but whose path is not System32.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parent from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name IN ("svchost.exe","lsass.exe","services.exe","taskhost.exe","taskhostw.exe","csrss.exe","smss.exe","wininit.exe","explorer.exe") AND NOT Processes.process_path IN ("*\\Windows\\System32\\*","*\\Windows\\SysWOW64\\*","*\\Windows\\WinSxS\\*") by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.process_name Processes.process_path Processes.process_guid | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | join type=inner process_guid [| tstats summariesonly=true count from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name="lsass.exe" OR (Processes.process="*sekurlsa*" OR Processes.process="*lsadump*" OR Processes.process="*MiniDump*" OR Processes.process="*comsvcs.dll*MiniDump*") by Processes.process_guid | rename Processes.process_guid as process_guid] | eval risk_reason="Masqueraded system-binary name running outside System32 with LSASS-dump activity — Whitefly Mimikatz tradecraft" | table firstTime lastTime dest user process_name process_path parent cmdline risk_reason | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)`
ucs.kql:
let WindowsCoreNames = dynamic(["svchost.exe","lsass.exe","services.exe","taskhost.exe","taskhostw.exe","csrss.exe","smss.exe","wininit.exe","explorer.exe","winlogon.exe"]);
let Masqueraded = DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(14d)
| where FileName in~ (WindowsCoreNames)
| where FolderPath !startswith "C:\\Windows\\System32\\" and FolderPath !startswith "C:\\Windows\\SysWOW64\\" and FolderPath !startswith "C:\\Windows\\WinSxS\\"
| project Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName, MasqProcessId=ProcessId, MasqProcessGuid=tostring(ProcessId), MasqFolderPath=FolderPath, MasqFileName=FileName, MasqCmd=ProcessCommandLine;
let LsassTouch = union
  (DeviceEvents | where ActionType in ("OpenProcessApiCall","ReadProcessMemoryApiCall") | where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (WindowsCoreNames) and InitiatingProcessFolderPath !startswith "C:\\Windows\\System32\\" | extend Trigger="LSASS handle" | project Timestamp, DeviceId, InitiatingProcessId, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessFolderPath, Trigger),
  (DeviceProcessEvents | where ProcessCommandLine has_any ("sekurlsa","lsadump","MiniDump","comsvcs.dll, MiniDump","procdump") and InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (WindowsCoreNames) | extend Trigger="Mimikatz/comsvcs cmdline" | project Timestamp, DeviceId, InitiatingProcessId, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessFolderPath, Trigger);
Masqueraded
| join kind=inner LsassTouch on DeviceId, $left.MasqProcessId == $right.InitiatingProcessId
| extend Detection="Whitefly tradecraft: system-binary masquerade (T1036.005) executing credential-dump primitive (T1003.001)"
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, MasqFileName, MasqFolderPath, MasqCmd, Trigger, Detection
ucs.rationale: Symantec's Whitefly write-up explicitly highlights the group's habit of disguising tooling (including Mimikatz variants) as Windows system binaries and dropping them in user-writable directories. Combining the masquerade signal (correct name, wrong path) with an LSASS-handle or Mimikatz-style command-line is a near-zero-false-positive primitive — legitimate svchost/lsass/services never execute out
